definitions.usage_example: They decided to break up last month.
definitions.alternative_example: They decided to end their relationship.
definitions.usage_example_es: Ellos decidieron terminar el mes pasado.

definitions.usage_example: When does school break up for summer?
definitions.alternative_example: When does school end for summer?
definitions.usage_example_es: ¿Cuándo terminan las clases por el verano?

definitions.usage_example: The party broke up at midnight.
definitions.alternative_example: The party ended at midnight.
definitions.usage_example_es: La fiesta se disolvió a medianoche.

definitions.usage_example: Don't bring up politics at the party.
definitions.alternative_example: Don't mention politics at the party.
definitions.usage_example_es: No saques el tema de la política en la fiesta.

definitions.usage_example: His grandparents brought him up.
definitions.alternative_example: His grandparents raised him.
definitions.usage_example_es: Sus abuelos lo criaron.

definitions.usage_example: We need to bring down our costs.
definitions.alternative_example: We need to reduce our costs.
definitions.usage_example_es: Necesitamos reducir nuestros costos.

definitions.usage_example: That sad movie really brought me down.
definitions.alternative_example: That sad movie really made me sad.
definitions.usage_example_es: Esa película triste realmente me deprimió.

definitions.usage_example: My new job brings in more money.
definitions.alternative_example: My new job earns more money.
definitions.usage_example_es: Mi nuevo trabajo genera más dinero.

definitions.usage_example: Let's bring in an expert to help us.
definitions.alternative_example: Let's involve an expert to help us.
definitions.usage_example_es: Traigamos a un experto para que nos ayude.

definitions.usage_example: The stress brought on a headache.
definitions.alternative_example: The stress caused a headache.
definitions.usage_example_es: El estrés me provocó un dolor de cabeza.

definitions.usage_example: You want a challenge? Bring it on!
definitions.alternative_example: You want a challenge? I accept!
definitions.usage_example_es: ¿Quieres un reto? ¡Adelante! (¡Venga!)

definitions.usage_example: We had to call off the game due to rain.
definitions.alternative_example: We had to cancel the game due to rain.
definitions.usage_example_es: Tuvimos que cancelar el partido debido a la lluvia.

definitions.usage_example: I can always call on my friends for help.
definitions.alternative_example: I can always ask my friends for help.
definitions.usage_example_es: Siempre puedo recurrir a mis amigos por ayuda.

definitions.usage_example: We should call on your grandma this weekend.
definitions.alternative_example: We should visit your grandma this weekend.
definitions.usage_example_es: Deberíamos visitar a tu abuela este fin de semana.

definitions.usage_example: This recipe calls for three eggs.
definitions.alternative_example: This recipe requires three eggs.
definitions.usage_example_es: Esta receta requiere tres huevos.

definitions.usage_example: The protesters are calling for change.
definitions.alternative_example: The protesters are demanding change.
definitions.usage_example_es: Los manifestantes están pidiendo un cambio.

definitions.usage_example: Please carry on with your work.
definitions.alternative_example: Please continue with your work.
definitions.usage_example_es: Por favor, continúa con tu trabajo.

definitions.usage_example: He was carrying on about the noise.
definitions.alternative_example: He was complaining loudly about the noise.
definitions.usage_example_es: Estaba haciendo un escándalo por el ruido.

definitions.meaning: Llevar a cabo, realizar (una tarea, un plan)
definitions.usage_example: The team will carry out the plan.
definitions.alternative_example: The team will execute the plan.
definitions.usage_example_es: El equipo llevará a cabo el plan.

definitions.meaning: Transferir (a un nuevo período o lugar)
definitions.usage_example: Will my vacation days carry over to next year?
definitions.alternative_example: Will my vacation days transfer to next year?
definitions.usage_example_es: ¿Mis días de vacaciones se transferirán al próximo año?

definitions.usage_example: Can you check over my report before I submit it?
definitions.alternative_example: Can you review my report before I submit it?
definitions.usage_example_es: ¿Puedes revisar mi informe antes de que lo entregue?

definitions.usage_example: I love to check off items on my to-do list.
definitions.alternative_example: I love to mark items as complete on my to-do list.
definitions.usage_example_es: Me encanta marcar tareas (ítems) en mi lista de pendientes.

definitions.usage_example: He came up with a great idea.
definitions.alternative_example: He thought of a great idea.
definitions.usage_example_es: Se le ocurrió una gran idea.

definitions.usage_example: You can come along with us.
definitions.alternative_example: You can come with us.
definitions.usage_example_es: Puedes venir con nosotros.

definitions.usage_example: I came across an old photo.
definitions.alternative_example: I found an old photo by chance.
definitions.usage_example_es: Encontré una foto vieja por casualidad.

definitions.usage_example: He comes across as friendly.
definitions.alternative_example: He seems friendly.
definitions.usage_example_es: Da la impresión de ser amigable.

definitions.usage_example: Something came up at work.
definitions.alternative_example: Something unexpected happened at work.
definitions.usage_example_es: Surgió algo en el trabajo.

definitions.meaning: Dejar (a alguien o algo) en un lugar
definitions.usage_example: Can you drop me off at the next corner?
definitions.alternative_example: Can you leave me at the next corner?
definitions.usage_example_es: ¿Puedes dejarme en la próxima esquina?

definitions.usage_example: Sales always drop off after Christmas.
definitions.alternative_example: Sales always decrease after Christmas.
definitions.usage_example_es: Las ventas siempre caen después de Navidad.

definitions.usage_example: I dropped off during the boring movie.
definitions.alternative_example: I fell asleep during the boring movie.
definitions.usage_example_es: Me quedé dormido durante la película aburrida.

definitions.usage_example: He dropped out of college after one semester.
definitions.alternative_example: He quit college after one semester.
definitions.usage_example_es: Él abandonó la universidad después de un semestre.

definitions.meaning: Visitar (a alguien) de pasada, sin avisar
definitions.usage_example: I'll drop by your office later today.
definitions.alternative_example: I'll visit your office informally later today.
definitions.usage_example_es: Pasaré por tu oficina más tarde hoy.

definitions.usage_example: Feel free to drop in anytime you're in town.
definitions.alternative_example: Feel free to visit informally anytime you're in town.
definitions.usage_example_es: Siéntete libre de pasar a visitar cuando estés en la ciudad.

definitions.usage_example: My friends and I get along really well. We’re always laughing.
definitions.alternative_example: My friends and I have a very good relationship. We’re always laughing.
definitions.usage_example_es: Mis amigos y yo nos llevamos muy bien, siempre nos estamos riendo.

definitions.usage_example: Despite the problems, they are somehow getting along.
definitions.alternative_example: Despite the problems, they are somehow managing.
definitions.usage_example_es: A pesar de los problemas, de alguna manera se las están arreglando.

definitions.usage_example: It took him months to get over his breakup.
definitions.alternative_example: It took him months to recover from his breakup.
definitions.usage_example_es: Le tomó meses superar su ruptura.

definitions.meaning: cruzar o pasar por encima de algo (físico)
definitions.usage_example: I need to get over this high wall.
definitions.alternative_example: I need to climb over this high wall.
definitions.usage_example_es: Necesito pasar por encima de este muro alto.

definitions.usage_example: We will get through this.
definitions.alternative_example: We will survive this situation.
definitions.usage_example_es: Vamos a superar esto.

definitions.usage_example: I couldn’t get through to the office.
definitions.alternative_example: I couldn’t reach the office.
definitions.usage_example_es: No pude comunicarme con la oficina.

definitions.usage_example: Don’t give up.
definitions.alternative_example: Don’t stop trying.
definitions.usage_example_es: No te rindas.

definitions.usage_example: He gave up smoking.
definitions.alternative_example: He stopped smoking.
definitions.usage_example_es: Él dejó de fumar.

definitions.usage_example: Please give back my phone.
definitions.alternative_example: Please return my phone.
definitions.usage_example_es: Por favor, devuélveme mi teléfono.

definitions.usage_example: She finally gave in.
definitions.alternative_example: She finally agreed after pressure.
definitions.usage_example_es: Ella finalmente cedió.

definitions.usage_example: They gave out water after the race.
definitions.alternative_example: They distributed water after the race.
definitions.usage_example_es: Repartieron agua después de la carrera.

definitions.usage_example: My legs gave out.
definitions.alternative_example: My legs stopped working properly.
definitions.usage_example_es: Mis piernas fallaron.

definitions.usage_example: Let’s go over the plan.
definitions.alternative_example: Let’s review the plan.
definitions.usage_example_es: Repasemos el plan.

definitions.usage_example: We went through a difficult time.
definitions.alternative_example: We experienced a difficult time.
definitions.usage_example_es: Pasamos por un momento difícil.

definitions.usage_example: Please go through the documents.
definitions.alternative_example: Please review the documents carefully.
definitions.usage_example_es: Por favor revisa los documentos.

definitions.usage_example: We like to hang out after class.
definitions.alternative_example: We like to spend time together after class.
definitions.usage_example_es: Nos gusta pasar el rato después de clase.

definitions.usage_example: I can’t keep up with you.
definitions.alternative_example: I can’t stay at your pace.
definitions.usage_example_es: No puedo seguirte el ritmo.

definitions.usage_example: Keep on trying.
definitions.alternative_example: Continue trying.
definitions.usage_example_es: Sigue intentándolo.

definitions.usage_example: Can you look after my dog?
definitions.alternative_example: Can you take care of my dog?
definitions.usage_example_es: ¿Puedes cuidar a mi perro?

definitions.usage_example: Look up the address online.
definitions.alternative_example: Search for the address online.
definitions.usage_example_es: Busca la dirección en internet.

definitions.usage_example: Things are looking up.
definitions.alternative_example: Things are improving.
definitions.usage_example_es: Las cosas están mejorando.

definitions.usage_example: The police will look into the case.
definitions.alternative_example: The police will investigate the case.
definitions.usage_example_es: La policía investigará el caso.

definitions.usage_example: Let’s move on.
definitions.alternative_example: Let’s continue.
definitions.usage_example_es: Sigamos adelante.

definitions.usage_example: I need to pick out a gift for my mom.
definitions.alternative_example: I need to choose a gift for my mom.
definitions.usage_example_es: Necesito elegir un regalo para mi mamá.

definitions.usage_example: He always picks on his little brother.
definitions.alternative_example: He always teases his little brother.
definitions.usage_example_es: Él siempre se mete con su hermano pequeño.

definitions.usage_example: She is just picking at her food.
definitions.alternative_example: She is eating her food without appetite.
definitions.usage_example_es: Ella solo está picoteando su comida.

definitions.usage_example: Never put off until tomorrow what you can do today.
definitions.alternative_example: Never postpone until tomorrow what you can do today.
definitions.usage_example_es: Nunca dejes para mañana lo que puedes hacer hoy.

definitions.usage_example: The bad smell put me off the food.
definitions.alternative_example: The bad smell discouraged me from eating the food.
definitions.usage_example_es: El mal olor me quitó las ganas de comer.

definitions.usage_example: I can't put up with his excuses anymore.
definitions.alternative_example: I can't tolerate his excuses anymore.
definitions.usage_example_es: Ya no puedo soportar sus excusas.

definitions.usage_example: You can put down your bag here.
definitions.alternative_example: You can place your bag here.
definitions.usage_example_es: Puedes dejar tu bolso aquí.

definitions.usage_example: He always puts down his coworkers.
definitions.alternative_example: He always insults his coworkers.
definitions.usage_example_es: Él siempre menosprecia a sus compañeros de trabajo.

definitions.usage_example: We had to put our old cat down.
definitions.alternative_example: We had to euthanize our old cat.
definitions.usage_example_es: Tuvimos que sacrificar a nuestro viejo gato.

definitions.usage_example: I ran into my old teacher at the store.
definitions.alternative_example: I met my old teacher unexpectedly at the store.
definitions.usage_example_es: Me encontré con mi antiguo profesor en la tienda.

definitions.usage_example: He ran into a tree with his bike.
definitions.alternative_example: He collided with a tree with his bike.
definitions.usage_example_es: Chocó contra un árbol con su bicicleta.

definitions.usage_example: A bus ran over his bicycle.
definitions.alternative_example: A bus hit his bicycle.
definitions.usage_example_es: Un autobús atropelló su bicicleta.

definitions.usage_example: The meeting ran over by 10 minutes.
definitions.alternative_example: The meeting exceeded the time by 10 minutes.
definitions.usage_example_es: La reunión se pasó 10 minutos.

definitions.usage_example: Let's run through the plan one more time.
definitions.alternative_example: Let's review the plan one more time.
definitions.usage_example_es: Repasemos el plan una vez más.

definitions.usage_example: Can I run this idea by you quickly?
definitions.alternative_example: Can I tell you this idea for your opinion?
definitions.usage_example_es: ¿Puedo consultarte esta idea rápidamente?

definitions.usage_example: We need to set up the chairs for the meeting.
definitions.alternative_example: We need to arrange the chairs for the meeting.
definitions.usage_example_es: Necesitamos montar (colocar) las sillas para la reunión.

definitions.usage_example: He said he was innocent and the police set him up.
definitions.alternative_example: He said he was innocent and the police framed him.
definitions.usage_example_es: Dijo que era inocente y que la policía le tendió una trampa.

definitions.meaning: Irse de viaje, ponerse en camino
definitions.usage_example: We set off early in the morning.
definitions.alternative_example: We started our journey early in the morning.
definitions.usage_example_es: Salimos (de viaje) temprano en la mañana.

definitions.usage_example: The smoke set off the fire alarm.
definitions.alternative_example: The smoke activated the fire alarm.
definitions.usage_example_es: El humo activó la alarma de incendios.

definitions.usage_example: He didn't show up for work today.
definitions.alternative_example: He didn't arrive at work today.
definitions.usage_example_es: Él no apareció (no vino) a trabajar hoy.

definitions.usage_example: You showed me up in front of my boss!
definitions.alternative_example: You embarrassed me in front of my boss!
definitions.usage_example_es: ¡Me hiciste quedar mal (me humillaste) frente a mi jefe!

definitions.usage_example: He's just showing off his new car.
definitions.alternative_example: He's just boasting about his new car.
definitions.usage_example_es: Él solo está presumiendo su auto nuevo.

definitions.usage_example: Let me show you around the new office.
definitions.alternative_example: Let me give you a tour of the new office.
definitions.usage_example_es: Déjame mostrarte la nueva oficina (hacerte un tour).

definitions.usage_example: I'm thinking of taking up yoga.
definitions.alternative_example: I'm thinking of starting yoga.
definitions.usage_example_es: Estoy pensando en empezar a hacer yoga.

definitions.usage_example: This new sofa takes up a lot of space.
definitions.alternative_example: This new sofa occupies a lot of space.
definitions.usage_example_es: Este nuevo sofá ocupa mucho espacio.

definitions.meaning: Asumir el control, tomar el relevo
definitions.usage_example: She will take over the company next year.
definitions.alternative_example: She will assume control of the company next year.
definitions.usage_example_es: Ella asumirá el control de la empresa el próximo año.

definitions.usage_example: You really take after your father.
definitions.alternative_example: You really resemble your father.
definitions.usage_example_es: Realmente te pareces a tu padre.

definitions.usage_example: I take back what I said. I was wrong.
definitions.alternative_example: I retract what I said. I was wrong.
definitions.usage_example_es: Retiro lo dicho. Estaba equivocado.

definitions.usage_example: I need to take back these shoes. They don't fit.
definitions.alternative_example: I need to return these shoes. They don't fit.
definitions.usage_example_es: Necesito devolver estos zapatos. No me quedan.

definitions.usage_example: Can you take down my phone number?
definitions.alternative_example: Can you write down my phone number?
definitions.usage_example_es: ¿Puedes anotar mi número de teléfono?

definitions.usage_example: It's time to take down the Christmas lights.
definitions.alternative_example: It's time to remove the Christmas lights.
definitions.usage_example_es: Es hora de quitar las luces de Navidad.

definitions.usage_example: Can you turn up the music? I love this song.
definitions.alternative_example: Can you increase the volume of the music?
definitions.usage_example_es: ¿Puedes subir la música? Me encanta esta canción.

definitions.usage_example: He finally turned up at the party at midnight.
definitions.alternative_example: He finally arrived at the party at midnight.
definitions.usage_example_es: Él finalmente apareció en la fiesta a medianoche.

definitions.usage_example: Could you turn down the TV? It's too loud.
definitions.alternative_example: Could you decrease the volume of the TV?
definitions.usage_example_es: ¿Podrías bajar la TV? Está muy alta.

definitions.usage_example: She turned down the job offer.
definitions.alternative_example: She rejected the job offer.
definitions.usage_example_es: Ella rechazó la oferta de trabajo.

definitions.usage_example: The caterpillar turned into a butterfly.
definitions.alternative_example: The caterpillar became a butterfly.
definitions.usage_example_es: La oruga se convirtió en mariposa.

definitions.usage_example: The movie turned out to be really good.
definitions.alternative_example: The movie was surprisingly really good.
definitions.usage_example_es: La película resultó ser muy buena.

definitions.usage_example: A lot of people turned out for the concert.
definitions.alternative_example: A lot of people attended the concert.
definitions.usage_example_es: Mucha gente acudió al concierto.

definitions.usage_example: Turn around and let me see your back.
definitions.alternative_example: Rotate and let me see your back.
definitions.usage_example_es: Date la vuelta y déjame ver tu espalda.

definitions.usage_example: I try to work out three times a week.
definitions.alternative_example: I try to exercise three times a week.
definitions.usage_example_es: Intento hacer ejercicio tres veces por semana.

definitions.usage_example: We need to work out a solution.
definitions.alternative_example: We need to find a solution.
definitions.usage_example_es: Necesitamos encontrar (resolver) una solución.

definitions.usage_example: I hope everything works out for you.
definitions.alternative_example: I hope everything goes well for you.
definitions.usage_example_es: Espero que todo te salga bien.

definitions.usage_example: What are you working on right now?
definitions.alternative_example: What project are you doing right now?
definitions.usage_example_es: ¿En qué estás trabajando ahora mismo?

definitions.usage_example: I need to work on my pronunciation.
definitions.alternative_example: I need to improve my pronunciation.
definitions.usage_example_es: Necesito trabajar en (mejorar) mi pronunciación.

definitions.usage_example: The feature is broken, but we can work around it.
definitions.alternative_example: The feature is broken, but we can find an alternative solution.
definitions.usage_example_es: La función está rota, pero podemos encontrar una alternativa.
